metadata.scopeLock: V1 lets external agent hosts create action intents for buy and cancel/recover flows, send users to Nooterra-hosted approval pages, receive scoped execution grants, submit evidence, finalize runs, issue receipts, and open disputes.
outOfScope: certified execution adapters and strict-domain browser fallback
metadata.ticketSizingRule: 8 is not allowed; split work before scheduling.
metadata.oneLineOperatingRule: Every ticket must make approval, grant, evidence, receipt, dispute, or operator recovery stronger.
metadata.sourceNote: This file is an external import artifact for Linear-style planning, not a replacement for the repo GitHub-first execution policy.

Exit Gate: dispute can be opened from receipt page
buy action can authorize capture and refund
cancel or recover path can close with proper receipt state
no uncapped spend possible in staging
install-to-first-approval under five minutes on the reference host
hosted approval deep links are stable
first partner can complete approval to receipt with host-owned execution
operators can recover the top failure modes from runbooks
no launch document or board implies Nooterra owns last-mile execution
100 percent receipt coverage for material actions
dispute flow works end-to-end without DB repair

epics.Title: Core domain model and state machines
epics.Description: Lock the launch object model, transition rules, hashing, idempotency, and event taxonomy before feature work branches.

epics.Title: Auth, identity, and approval security
epics.Description: Ship passkey-first identity, approval-link hardening, host trust records, and revocation paths that fail closed.

epics.Title: Public API
epics.Description: Expose the host-first Action Wallet lifecycle through a bounded public API with auth, idempotency, and docs.

epics.Title: Action Wallet and policy engine
epics.Description: Bind approval, standing rules, policy evaluation, and grant semantics into one bounded wallet envelope.

epics.Title: Payments and settlement
epics.Description: Launch one managed payment path, delay capture until verification passes, and bind money state into receipts.

epics.Title: Evidence and verification
epics.Description: Define evidence contracts, verify runs, generate receipts, and surface insufficiency or override paths.

epics.Title: Consumer web surfaces
epics.Description: Ship the wallet, approvals, receipts, disputes, integrations, and blocked-state surfaces users actually touch.

epics.Title: Operator console
epics.Description: Give operators the queue, detail, rescue, dispute, and audit surfaces needed to keep launch runs supportable.

epics.Title: Host pack
epics.Description: Package the public API through MCP, OpenClaw, SDKs, CLI, and reference integrations that get hosts to first approval fast.

epics.Title: Observability, security, and reliability
epics.Description: Instrument, alert, review, smoke-test, and harden the system so launch reliability is measurable and reversible.

epics.Title: Docs, launch, and support
epics.Description: Reset the narrative, publish the API and integration docs, and prepare launch, support, and post-launch operating artifacts.

tickets.Title: Freeze v1 object model
tickets.Why: Locking the core objects early prevents silent contract drift across API, UI, and verification work.
Scope: Define Action Intent through Settlement Event as the launch object set
Pin schema names, field ownership, and persistence mappings
Record unresolved object questions and close them in Sprint 0
Out of Scope: New object families beyond the nine launch objects
Acceptance Criteria: Schema doc exists for all launch objects
DB mappings exist for each locked object
No unresolved field questions remain
tickets.Area: Core domain model and state machines
tickets.Rollback Note: Ship behind versioned schema or API gates and keep old read paths until backfill and smoke coverage pass.

tickets.Title: Define intent state machine
tickets.Why: Intent state drift will break approvals, execution, and receipts unless transitions are explicit and enforced.
Scope: Define draft through cancelled states
Block invalid transitions at the domain layer
Document the state diagram and transition log format
Out of Scope: Ad hoc state changes in handlers
Extra launch states beyond the locked set
Acceptance Criteria: Invalid transitions are blocked
Every transition is logged
State diagram is documented
tickets.Area: Core domain model and state machines
tickets.Rollback Note: Ship behind versioned schema or API gates and keep old read paths until backfill and smoke coverage pass.

tickets.Title: Define approval state machine
tickets.Why: Approval links and decisions need a single lifecycle so hosts and users read the same truth.
Scope: Define pending through revoked states
Specify expiry and revocation behavior
Align decision writes to the approval lifecycle
Out of Scope: Reusable links beyond the launch rules
Acceptance Criteria: Approval states are locked to pending, approved, denied, expired, and revoked
Expiry and revocation rules are explicit
Decision writes use the same state model
tickets.Area: Core domain model and state machines
tickets.Rollback Note: Ship behind versioned schema or API gates and keep old read paths until backfill and smoke coverage pass.

tickets.Title: Define execution grant semantics
tickets.Why: Grants are the core authority envelope; unclear semantics create uncapped or out-of-scope execution risk.
Scope: Lock principal, action type, host, allowlist, spend cap, expiry, evidence requirements, nonce, and lineage reference fields
Define grant validation semantics
Specify how grants bind back to approvals
Out of Scope: Broad delegated authority outside launch actions
Acceptance Criteria: All listed fields are defined
Grant validation semantics are documented
Approval binding rules are explicit
tickets.Area: Core domain model and state machines
tickets.Rollback Note: Ship behind versioned schema or API gates and keep old read paths until backfill and smoke coverage pass.

tickets.Title: Define receipt semantics
tickets.Why: The launch promise collapses if receipts do not bind approval, grant, evidence, settlement, and dispute state.
Scope: Define receipt bindings to approval, grant, evidence, settlement, verifier verdict, dispute state, and deterministic hash
Specify required receipt fields for both launch actions
Document receipt read model expectations
Out of Scope: Receipt fields not tied to a source object
Acceptance Criteria: Receipt schema binds all required objects
Required launch fields are documented
Deterministic hash rules are defined

tickets.Rollback Note: Ship behind versioned schema or API gates and keep old read paths until backfill and smoke coverage pass.

tickets.Title: Define dispute lifecycle

Document resolution end states

Acceptance Criteria: Dispute states are locked

Resolution end states are explicit

tickets.Title: Define idempotency model

Specify collision and replay responses
Document storage and expiry expectations
Acceptance Criteria: Create and finalize endpoints require idempotency support
Replay behavior is documented

tickets.Title: Define deterministic hashing

Document canonical serialization rules
Specify versioning for future hash changes
Acceptance Criteria: All four launch objects have stable semantic hashes
Canonical serialization rules are documented
